mappings.rationale: Directly addresses British interwar depression, WWII under Churchill, the welfare state, and Conservative/Labour governments discussed in A.1.a.
Examines British political developments including party politics, coalition governments, and constitutional change discussed in A.1.a.
Directly addresses Catholic-Protestant antagonism, the IRA, continuing violence, and the Belfast Agreement discussed in A.1.b.ii.
Traces the division of Ireland, the Irish Free State, De Valera's governments, and subsequent political developments discussed in A.1.b.i.
Traces France from the Third Republic through de Gaulle, the Fifth Republic, and Mitterrand discussed in A.2.
Examines the Weimar Republic's collapse, the Third Reich, Hitler's totalitarian state, and the persecution of Jews discussed in A.3.a.
Germany's reaction to the Treaty of Versailles and the reparations crisis destabilized the Weimar Republic as discussed in A.3.a.i.
Directly addresses Italian Fascism, Mussolini, postwar recovery, terrorism, and the Berlusconi era discussed in A.4.
Traces the Fascist Party's rise in Italy under Mussolini, Franco's Spain, and Salazar's Portugal discussed in A.4 and A.5.
Directly addresses the Civil War, German and Italian intervention, and Franco's victory discussed in A.5.a.i.
Examines the welfare state in Britain (A.1.a.iv), Scandinavian welfare states (A.6.b), and social welfare programs across western Europe.
Traces Labour and Social Democratic parties in Britain and Scandinavia, and socialist movements across Europe discussed throughout A.
Traces the Soviet state from the October Revolution through Gorbachev's glasnost and perestroika to the USSR's dissolution in B.1.
Explores the October Revolution, Civil War, War Communism, and the New Economic Policy discussed in B.1.a.i.
Examines Russia after the collapse of the Soviet Union including the Yeltsin and Putin eras relevant to B.1.c.
Addresses Soviet economic issues including industrialization, agricultural problems, and the economic crisis under Gorbachev in B.1.
Examines the Stalinist purges, forced labor, and political repression discussed in B.1.a.ii.
Explores communist governments in eastern Europe and their eventual collapse discussed in B.1-3.
Provides background for the successor states of Austria, Hungary, and Czechoslovakia discussed in B.2.
Traces the European Economic Community, Common Market, and EU expansion including eastern European accession discussed throughout.
Directly addresses postwar European recovery, Cold War division, the end of European empires, and social transformations -- the core subject of this section.
Explores the division of Europe, NATO, the Berlin Wall, and the end of the Cold War discussed throughout B.1-3.
Examines the wartime experiences of European nations including occupation, resistance, and liberation discussed throughout.

mappings.vsiTitle: The Great Depression and The New Deal
mappings.rationale: The Great Depression's devastation reshaped European economies including Germany, France, Scandinavia, and the Balkans.
The Holocaust and concentration camp system under Nazi Germany are central to A.3.a.ii.
Examines the persecution of Jews under Nazi Germany and broader European antisemitism discussed in A.3.a.ii.
Explores Basque separatism, Scottish and Welsh devolution, the Walloon-Fleming division, and ethnic conflicts in the Balkans.
Examines the Hungarian Revolution (1956), the Prague Spring (1968), and the 1989 revolutions across eastern Europe in B.2.
Addresses the Spanish Civil War, Greek civil war (1946-49), and Yugoslav wars of the 1990s in A.5.a, B.3.b.i, and B.3.b.iii.
Examines Franco's Spain, Salazar's Portugal, the Greek military dictatorship, and communist dictatorships in eastern Europe.
Explores democratic transitions in eastern Europe and the fall of communist regimes discussed throughout B.2-3.
Addresses Belgian federalization along communal lines and federal arrangements in Germany discussed in A.7 and A.3.
Scottish national identity and the devolution movement are relevant to the devolved assembly for Scotland in A.1.a.iv.
Explores displacement during WWII, the flight of East Germans, and Balkan refugee crises.
Addresses terrorism in Northern Ireland and the Yugoslav conflicts discussed in A.1.b.ii and B.3.b.iii.
Provides broader Russian historical context for understanding Soviet and post-Soviet developments in B.1.
